machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Named as part of the VirB2-11 apparatus set in Fromm et al. 2024 (Dehio lab, B. henselae VirB/VirD4 T4SS). Schulein & Dehio 2002 independently define the operon as virB2-11. Major-pilin role from the VirB/D4 apparatus brief vocabulary; B. henselae lacks virB1, so virB2 is the first gene of the operon.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Named within the VirB2-11 apparatus range (Fromm 2024); also covered by the virB2-11 locus definition in Schulein & Dehio 2002. IM/pilus-assembly role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: A generic method for marker-less gene replacement allowed the generation of non-polar in-frame deletions in either virB4 or virD4. Both mutants were unable to cause bacteraemia
machinery_genes.note: Schulein & Dehio 2002 individually delete virB4 in Bartonella and show it is essential for infection, establishing it as a bona fide VirB/VirD4 apparatus component. ATPase/energiser role from apparatus brief. Also within the VirB2-11 set named by Fromm 2024.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Within the VirB2-11 apparatus set (Fromm 2024); also covered by virB2-11 locus in Schulein & Dehio 2002. Minor-pilin role from apparatus brief. VirB5 is one of the two VirB proteins explicitly tabulated in the Engel 2011 parallel-evolution analysis of the Bartonella virB operon.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Within the VirB2-11 apparatus set (Fromm 2024); also covered by virB2-11 locus in Schulein & Dehio 2002. Polytopic IM channel role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Within the VirB2-11 apparatus set (Fromm 2024); also covered by virB2-11 locus in Schulein & Dehio 2002. OM-lipoprotein core-complex role from apparatus brief. VirB7 is the other VirB protein explicitly tabulated in the Engel 2011 Bartonella virB analysis.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Within the VirB2-11 apparatus set (Fromm 2024); also covered by virB2-11 locus in Schulein & Dehio 2002. IM assembly-factor role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Within the VirB2-11 apparatus set (Fromm 2024); also covered by virB2-11 locus in Schulein & Dehio 2002. OM core-complex role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Within the VirB2-11 apparatus set (Fromm 2024); also covered by virB2-11 locus in Schulein & Dehio 2002. IM-OM channel-ring role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4. The VirB proteins assemble a large macromolecular translocation channel.
machinery_genes.note: Within the VirB2-11 apparatus set (Fromm 2024); virB11 is the terminal gene of the virB2-11 locus in Schulein & Dehio 2002. Cytoplasmic-ATPase role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: the virB locus (virB2-11) and the downstream located virD4 gene, which together encode a putative type IV secretion system (T4SS)... non-polar in-frame deletions in either virB4 or virD4. Both mutants were unable to cause bacteraemia
machinery_genes.note: Schulein & Dehio 2002 individually delete virD4 in Bartonella and show it is essential. Fromm 2024 names it as the T4S coupling protein: 'Substrates of the T4SS are recruited by the T4SS-associated ATPase VirD4, also termed T4S coupling protein (T4CP).' Coupling-protein/receptor-ATPase role; encoded downstream of the virB2-11 operon.

notes: System = the Bartonella henselae VirB/VirD4 type-IVA secretion system (VirB/D4 archetype), the chromosomally encoded machine that translocates the Bartonella effector proteins (BepA-G, this instance's curated effectors BH13370-BH13440) into human/endothelial host cells. The apparatus and the bep genes lie together on a Bartonella-specific pathogenicity island. Core apparatus is the VirB2-VirB11 channel plus the VirD4 coupling-protein ATPase. Two literature sources define the gene set: Schulein & Dehio 2002 (Mol Microbiol), the founding genetic paper, defines 'the virB locus (virB2-11) and the downstream located virD4 gene' and individually deletes virB4 and virD4 (both essential for intraerythrocytic infection); Fromm et al. 2024 (PNAS, Dehio lab, open access) restates 'The VirB/VirD4 T4SS is composed of VirB2-11 and VirD4' and names VirD4 as the T4S coupling protein. Bartonella lacks virB1 (lytic transglycosylase), so the operon runs virB2 through virB11; no virB1 listed. Per-gene apparatus roles (pilin, ATPase, OM core complex, etc.) are assigned from the VirB/D4 apparatus brief vocabulary, with virB4 and virD4 additionally supported by direct functional deletion quotes. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the translocated effectors (Beps / BepA-G, ByeA), the C-terminal BID transfer signal, and the trimeric autotransporter adhesin BadA (a separate adhesin, not part of the VirB channel). Lowest-confidence point: virB3 and virB6-virB10 are not individually quoted by name; they are covered only by the inclusive 'VirB2-11 / virB2-11' range statements from the two apparatus papers, which is standard usage for this canonical operon. RefSeq locus tags (BH... in NC_005956.1) were NOT mined; gene-to-coordinate resolution is a later scripted step.
